purpose: Direct LLM applicability check for a reference_only rule that needs scope reasoning.

rule.title: Prefer minimizing time in critical sections rather than growing lock scopes around unrelated work
rule.summary: Concurrency design guidance is often most useful as a review reference unless the patch shows a concrete blocking or callback hazard.
rule.rule_text: Use this when the change suggests lock-scope drift, expensive work under a mutex, or ownership confusion around callbacks, but the patch alone does not prove deadlock.

draft_under_test.title: 잠금 범위 안에서 네트워크 호출과 콜백이 실행됩니다
draft_under_test.summary: 이 변경은 mutex를 잡은 상태에서 네트워크 refresh와 subscriber notification을 수행합니다. 잠금이 I/O와 콜백까지 확장되면 다른 호출자가 오래 막히거나 callback-under-lock 위험이 생기므로 critical section을 상태 변경에만 좁히는지 검토해야 합니다.
draft_under_test.suggested_fix: 상태 변경만 mutex 안에서 수행하고, 네트워크 호출과 콜백은 잠금 해제 후 실행하도록 분리합니다.

results.why_llm_needed: lock, network call, and callback all appear together; applicability depends on recognizing they execute while the guard is still in scope.
the same words appear nearby, but scope braces show the network call and callback happen after unlocking.

notes: This directly exercises provider.verify_draft, not the full auto-publish lifecycle.
Current retrieval/runner policy still suppresses reference_only rules before drafting/publishing.
The stub verifier returns applies=true and cannot distinguish the negative contrast case.
